title: Developmental cell programs are co-opted in inflammatory skin disease
quotes:
Three fibroblast subsets expressing extracellular matrix (ECM)-related genes such as
MMP2, COL1A1, COL1A2 and NT5E (encodes CD73) are present in healthy human skin,
dominated by Fb1 fibroblasts with minor populations of Fb2 and Fb3 fibroblasts

Dermal fibroblast heterogeneity encompassing structural and immunomodulatory
subtypes has been previously reported at single cell level
summary: Reynolds et al. (2021) identify Fb3 (F3) as a minor dermal fibroblast population in healthy adult skin. Like Fb1 and Fb2, it expresses ECM-related genes (MMP2, COL1A1, COL1A2, NT5E/CD73). Its distinct transcriptional profile from Fb1 and Fb2 places it as a third fibroblast state, though its specific functions in healthy dermis are less characterised than Fb2.

title: Single-cell transcriptomes of the human skin reveal age-related loss of fibroblast priming
quotes: Our results define four main subpopulations that can be spatially localized and show differential secretory, mesenchymal and pro-inflammatory functional annotations.
we found that this fibroblast 'priming' becomes reduced with age
summary: Sole-Boldo et al. (2020) provide independent characterisation of human dermal fibroblast subpopulations with distinct spatial, functional and age-related properties, providing context for the minor Fb3/F3 subtype identified by Reynolds et al.
